title: oenothera glauca var. fraseri
intro: Oenothera glauca var. fraseri, also known as the Fraser's Evening Primrose, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Onagraceae, characterized by its white, fragrant flowers and narrow, pointed leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Oenothera glauca var. fraseri include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve skin health, and boost the immune system. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations such as infusions, salves, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
